revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2024年1月}}
[[画像:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|奈良市西紀寺町 崇道天皇社]] '''崇道天皇神社'''（すどうてんのうじんじゃ）は、[[早良親王]]（崇道天皇）を主祭神とする神社で、奈良から西日本を中心に各地に存在する{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}。'''崇道天皇社'''（すどうてんのうしゃ)、'''崇道神社'''（すどうじんじゃ）等と呼称する場合や、｢ソウドウ」と読み、'''宗道神社'''（そうどうじんじゃ)、'''惣戸神社'''（そうどじんじゃ）等の字をあてる例もある{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}。
== 由緒 == [[藤原種継]]暗殺連座の疑いを受け、[[淡路国]]配流の途上[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]され、その後祟りをなしたとされる早良親王を祀る神社であり、[[御霊信仰|御霊社]]としての性格を強く持つ。[[淡路国]]から[[大和国]]八島に改葬された早良親王の[[陵墓]]である[[八島陵]]には、[[1886年]]（[[明治]]19年）まで早良親王を祀り慰める[[八島陵#崇道天皇社|崇道天皇社]]が存在したが、同一の郷に属する近隣の町内にはそこから[[分霊|勧請]]したとみられる小社が残されている{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}。奈良市西紀寺町の[[崇道天皇社]]の場合は、[[奈良|南都]][[御霊会]]で用いられた[[御霊会#八所御霊|八所御霊]]の[[神輿]]のうち、崇道天皇の神輿舎があった場所に成立した神社とみられている{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>[[村井古道#奈良坊目拙解|奈良坊目拙解]]</ref>。
地方、特に[[山陽地方]]に残る崇道神社あるいはソウドウ社においては、[[延暦]]24年（[[805年]]）、朝廷より諸国に命じて郡ごとに作らせた崇道天皇御倉が元となり、倉の廃絶後、後世神社となったとする説がある{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">『日本後紀』延暦二十四年四月甲辰条</ref><ref name="ruiju">類従三代格</ref>。崇道天皇御倉とは、諸国郡に初穂40束を正税として納めさせ、早良親王御霊の鎮撫のために毎年積立させるために設けた正倉であるという{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki" /><ref name="ruiju" />{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>大鏡</ref><ref>大安寺崇道天皇御院八島両処記文</ref>。
逆に崇道神社を名乗りながら、早良親王が祀られない事例も存在する。[[嘉吉]]元年（[[1441年]]）の『[[興福寺官務牒疏]]』によると、[[山城国]][[相楽郡]]土師郷に鎮座した崇道天王神は、祭神が[[舎人親王]]であったという<ref>興福寺官務牒疏</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}。舎人親王の[[諡|追尊号]]が'''崇道尽敬皇帝'''であったことによる省略、取り違えであろうと考えられる{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}。
== 主な崇道天皇神社 ==
=== 崇道天皇神社 ===
* [[崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|崇道天皇神社]] – [[兵庫県]][[たつの市]]揖保町 ： 旧郷社 * [[八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]出屋敷町 * [[八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – 奈良県奈良市神殿町 * [[八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – 奈良県奈良市北永井町
=== 崇道天皇社 ===
* [[崇道天皇社]] – 奈良県奈良市西紀寺町 ： 旧村社 * [[八島陵#崇道天皇社|崇道天皇社]] – 奈良県奈良市八島町 ： 崇道天皇陵 ([[八島陵]]) 上にかつてあった神社。明治に[[嶋田神社]]に合祀。 * [[加茂神社 (津山市)|加茂神社]] – [[岡山県]][[津山市]]加茂町 ： 往古「杉大明神社」だったが、御陵があるので「崇道天皇社」に改称したという。実際に本殿裏山中腹には陵墓と伝えられる塚がある。
== 主な崇道神社 ==
=== 崇道神社 ===
* [[崇道神社]] – [[京都府]][[京都市]][[左京区]]上高野 ： 旧村社 * [[崇道神社 (御所市)|崇道神社]] – 奈良県[[御所市]]櫛羅 * [[崇道神社 (井原市)|崇道神社]] – 岡山県[[井原市]]西江原町 * [[崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|崇道神社]] – 岡山県[[小田郡]][[矢掛町]]里山田 * [[崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|崇道神社]] – [[広島県]][[神石郡]][[神石高原町]]小畠
=== その他 ===
* [[崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|崇導神社]] – 兵庫県[[揖保郡]][[太子町 (兵庫県)|太子町]]福地 ： 主祭神は舎人親王 * [[崇堂神社]] – 広島県[[福山市]]大門町 ： 主祭神は岡志摩守景勝
== 主な宗道神社・惣戸神社 ==
=== 宗道神社 === * [[宗道神社 (下妻市)|宗道神社]] – [[茨城県]][[下妻市]]宗道 ： 主祭神は建速須佐男命 ([[スサノオ]]) * [[宗道神社 (津山市)|宗道神社]] – [[岡山県]][[津山市]]鍛治町<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社 {{!}} 岡山県北の生活情報 アットタウンWEBマガジン |url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100 |accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref>
=== 惣戸神社 === * [[惣戸神社]] – [[広島県]][[福山市]]南蔵王町
=== その他 === * [[総道神社]] – 兵庫県[[宍粟市]]山崎町 ： 主祭神は猿田彦神 ([[サルタヒコ]]) * [[惣堂明神社]] – 広島県福山市津之郷町津之郷1731 ： 主祭神は[[足利義昭]] * [[上惣堂神社]] – 広島県福山市津之郷町津之郷1372 ： 主祭神は足利義昭
== 出典 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|title=〈小さき社〉の列島史|editor=[[牛山佳幸]]|publisher=平凡社|year=2000|isbn=978-4582842036|ref={{sfnref|小さき社の列島史}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[御霊信仰]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Emperor Sudo Shrines}}

* [[崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|崇道天皇神社]] – [[兵庫県]][[たつの市]]揖保町 ： 旧郷社 * [[八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]出屋敷町 * [[八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – 奈良県奈良市神殿町 * [[八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – 奈良県奈良市北永井町
=== 崇道天皇社 ===
* [[崇道天皇社]] – 奈良県奈良市西紀寺町 ： 旧村社 * [[八島陵#崇道天皇社|崇道天皇社]] – 奈良県奈良市八島町 ： 崇道天皇陵 ([[八島陵]]) 上にかつてあった神社。明治に[[嶋田神社]]に合祀。 * [[加茂神社 (津山市)|加茂神社]] – [[岡山県]][[津山市]]加茂町 ： 往古「杉大明神社」だったが、御陵があるので「崇道天皇社」に改称したという。実際に本殿裏山中腹には陵墓と伝えられる塚がある。
== 主な崇道神社 ==
=== 崇道神社 ===
* [[崇道神社]] – [[京都府]][[京都市]][[左京区]]上高野 ： 旧村社 * [[崇道神社 (御所市)|崇道神社]] – 奈良県[[御所市]]櫛羅 * [[崇道神社 (井原市)|崇道神社]] – 岡山県[[井原市]]西江原町 * [[崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|崇道神社]] – 岡山県[[小田郡]][[矢掛町]]里山田 * [[崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|崇道神社]] – [[広島県]][[神石郡]][[神石高原町]]小畠
=== その他 ===
* [[崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|崇導神社]] – 兵庫県[[揖保郡]][[太子町 (兵庫県)|太子町]]福地 ： 主祭神は舎人親王 * [[崇堂神社]] – 広島県[[福山市]]大門町 ： 主祭神は岡志摩守景勝
== 主な宗道神社・惣戸神社 ==
=== 宗道神社 === * [[宗道神社 (下妻市)|宗道神社]] – [[茨城県]][[下妻市]]宗道 ： 主祭神は建速須佐男命 ([[スサノオ]]) * [[宗道神社 (津山市)|宗道神社]] – [[岡山県]][[津山市]]鍛治町<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社 {{!}} 岡山県北の生活情報 アットタウンWEBマガジン |url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100 |accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref>
=== 惣戸神社 === * [[惣戸神社]] – [[広島県]][[福山市]]南蔵王町
=== その他 === * [[総道神社]] – 兵庫県[[宍粟市]]山崎町 ： 主祭神は猿田彦神 ([[サルタヒコ]]) * [[惣堂明神社]] – 広島県福山市津之郷町津之郷1731 ： 主祭神は[[足利義昭]] * [[上惣堂神社]] – 広島県福山市津之郷町津之郷1372 ： 主祭神は足利義昭
== 出典 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|title=〈小さき社〉の列島史|editor=[[牛山佳幸]]|publisher=平凡社|year=2000|isbn=978-4582842036|ref={{sfnref|小さき社の列島史}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[御霊信仰]]
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Emperor Sudo Shrines}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[崇道天皇神社]] to [[Sudo Tenno Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2024年1月}}
[[画像:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|奈良市西紀寺町 崇道天皇社]] '''崇道天皇神社'''（すどうてんのうじんじゃ）は、[[早良親王]]（崇道天皇）を主祭神とする神社で、奈良から西日本を中心に各地に存在する{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}。'''崇道天皇社'''（すどうてんのうしゃ)、'''崇道神社'''（すどうじんじゃ）等と呼称する場合や、｢ソウドウ」と読み、'''宗道神社'''（そうどうじんじゃ)、'''惣戸神社'''（そうどじんじゃ）等の字をあてる例もある{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}。
== 由緒 == [[藤原種継]]暗殺連座の疑いを受け、[[淡路国]]配流の途上[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]され、その後祟りをなしたとされる早良親王を祀る神社であり、[[御霊信仰|御霊社]]としての性格を強く持つ。[[淡路国]]から[[大和国]]八島に改葬された早良親王の[[陵墓]]である[[八島陵]]には、[[1886年]]（[[明治]]19年）まで早良親王を祀り慰める[[八島陵#崇道天皇社|崇道天皇社]]が存在したが、同一の郷に属する近隣の町内にはそこから[[分霊|勧請]]したとみられる小社が残されている{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}。奈良市西紀寺町の[[崇道天皇社]]の場合は、[[奈良|南都]][[御霊会]]で用いられた[[御霊会#八所御霊|八所御霊]]の[[神輿]]のうち、崇道天皇の神輿舎があった場所に成立した神社とみられている{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>[[村井古道#奈良坊目拙解|奈良坊目拙解]]</ref>。
地方、特に[[山陽地方]]に残る崇道神社あるいはソウドウ社においては、[[延暦]]24年（[[805年]]）、朝廷より諸国に命じて郡ごとに作らせた崇道天皇御倉が元となり、倉の廃絶後、後世神社となったとする説がある{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">『日本後紀』延暦二十四年四月甲辰条</ref><ref name="ruiju">類従三代格</ref>。崇道天皇御倉とは、諸国郡に初穂40束を正税として納めさせ、早良親王御霊の鎮撫のために毎年積立させるために設けた正倉であるという{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki" /><ref name="ruiju" />{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>大鏡</ref><ref>大安寺崇道天皇御院八島両処記文</ref>。
逆に崇道神社を名乗りながら、早良親王が祀られない事例も存在する。[[嘉吉]]元年（[[1441年]]）の『[[興福寺官務牒疏]]』によると、[[山城国]][[相楽郡]]土師郷に鎮座した崇道天王神は、祭神が[[舎人親王]]であったという<ref>興福寺官務牒疏</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}。舎人親王の[[諡|追尊号]]が'''崇道尽敬皇帝'''であったことによる省略、取り違えであろうと考えられる{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}。
== 主な崇道天皇神社 ==
=== 崇道天皇神社 ===
* [[崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|崇道天皇神社]] – [[兵庫県]][[たつの市]]揖保町 ： 旧郷社 * [[八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]出屋敷町 * [[八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – 奈良県奈良市神殿町 * [[八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|崇道天皇神社]] – 奈良県奈良市北永井町
=== 崇道天皇社 ===
* [[崇道天皇社]] – 奈良県奈良市西紀寺町 ： 旧村社 * [[八島陵#崇道天皇社|崇道天皇社]] – 奈良県奈良市八島町 ： 崇道天皇陵 ([[八島陵]]) 上にかつてあった神社。明治に[[嶋田神社]]に合祀。 * [[加茂神社 (津山市)|加茂神社]] – [[岡山県]][[津山市]]加茂町 ： 往古「杉大明神社」だったが、御陵があるので「崇道天皇社」に改称したという。実際に本殿裏山中腹には陵墓と伝えられる塚がある。
== 主な崇道神社 ==
=== 崇道神社 ===
* [[崇道神社]] – [[京都府]][[京都市]][[左京区]]上高野 ： 旧村社 * [[崇道神社 (御所市)|崇道神社]] – 奈良県[[御所市]]櫛羅 * [[崇道神社 (井原市)|崇道神社]] – 岡山県[[井原市]]西江原町 * [[崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|崇道神社]] – 岡山県[[小田郡]][[矢掛町]]里山田 * [[崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|崇道神社]] – [[広島県]][[神石郡]][[神石高原町]]小畠
=== その他 ===
* [[崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|崇導神社]] – 兵庫県[[揖保郡]][[太子町 (兵庫県)|太子町]]福地 ： 主祭神は舎人親王 * [[崇堂神社]] – 広島県[[福山市]]大門町 ： 主祭神は岡志摩守景勝
== 主な宗道神社・惣戸神社 ==
=== 宗道神社 === * [[宗道神社 (下妻市)|宗道神社]] – [[茨城県]][[下妻市]]宗道 ： 主祭神は建速須佐男命 ([[スサノオ]]) * [[宗道神社 (津山市)|宗道神社]] – [[岡山県]][[津山市]]鍛治町<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社 {{!}} 岡山県北の生活情報 アットタウンWEBマガジン |url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100 |accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref>
=== 惣戸神社 === * [[惣戸神社]] – [[広島県]][[福山市]]南蔵王町
=== その他 === * [[総道神社]] – 兵庫県[[宍粟市]]山崎町 ： 主祭神は猿田彦神 ([[サルタヒコ]]) * [[惣堂明神社]] – 広島県福山市津之郷町津之郷1731 ： 主祭神は[[足利義昭]] * [[上惣堂神社]] – 広島県福山市津之郷町津之郷1372 ： 主祭神は足利義昭
== 出典 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|title=〈小さき社〉の列島史|editor=[[牛山佳幸]]|publisher=平凡社|year=2000|isbn=978-4582842036|ref={{sfnref|小さき社の列島史}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[御霊信仰]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|qq=|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City
=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|qq=|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|qq=|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City
=== Hyōgo Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja|崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Taishi, Ibo County; Main deity: Prince Toneri * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja|崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tatsuno City * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja|ja|総道神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shisō City; Main deity: {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiaki|Ashikaga Yoshiaki]] * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[commons:" to "[[sn:commons:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|qq=|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City
=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|qq=|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|qq=|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|qq=|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City
=== Hyōgo Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja|崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Taishi, Ibo County; Main deity: Prince Toneri * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja|崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tatsuno City * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja|ja|総道神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shisō City; Main deity: {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiaki|Ashikaga Yoshiaki]] * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County

=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|qq=|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City

=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|qq=|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City
=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City
=== Hyōgo Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja|崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Taishi, Ibo County; Main deity: Prince Toneri * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja|崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tatsuno City * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja|ja|総道神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shisō City; Main deity: {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiaki|Ashikaga Yoshiaki]] * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County

=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City

=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=draft|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City
=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City
=== Hyōgo Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja|崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Taishi, Ibo County; Main deity: Prince Toneri * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja|崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tatsuno City * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja|ja|総道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shisō City; Main deity: {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiaki|Ashikaga Yoshiaki]] * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City
=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City
=== Hyōgo Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja|崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Taishi, Ibo County; Main deity: Prince Toneri * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja|崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tatsuno City * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja|ja|総道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shisō City; Main deity: {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiaki|Ashikaga Yoshiaki]] * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiaki|Ashikaga Yoshiaki]] * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City
=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City
=== Hyōgo Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja|崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Taishi, Ibo County; Main deity: Prince Toneri * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja|崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tatsuno City * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja|ja|総道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shisō City; Main deity: {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiaki|Ashikaga Yoshiaki]] * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=〈小さき社〉の列島史|editor=Ushiyama Yoshiyuki|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=978-4582842036|ref={{sfnref|小さき社の列島史}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24876234]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]]. He passed away during exile in [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the [[:en:Goryō|Goryō faith]]. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]], the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], venerates [[:en:Prince Toneri|Prince Toneri]]<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City
=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|1=Yashima no Misasagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City
=== Hyōgo Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja|崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Taishi, Ibo County; Main deity: Prince Toneri * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja|崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tatsuno City * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja|ja|総道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shisō City; Main deity: {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshiaki|Ashikaga Yoshiaki]] * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
