revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]]
{{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} primarily dedicated to {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|1=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}}. He passed away during exile in {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}, and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=Goryō faith|1=Goryō}}. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima Mausoleum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima Mausoleum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}}, the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, venerates {{ill|Prince Toneri|en|Prince Toneri|WD=Q1362200|lt=Prince Toneri|1=Prince Toneri}}<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
=== Kyoto Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja|ja|崇道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}} – Kamitakano, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto City
=== Nara Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (abolished)|1=Yashima Mausoleum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Formerly atop Yashima Tomb; now merged with {{ill|Shimada Jinja|ja|嶋田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Jinja|1=Shimada Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876134}} * {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha|1=Sōdō Emperor Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} – Nishikidera-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|ja|八島陵#出屋敷町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Deyashiki)|1=Yashima Mausoleum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Deyashiki-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|ja|八島陵#神殿町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kodono)|1=Yashima Mausoleum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kodono-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|ja|八島陵#北永井町 崇道天皇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Kitanagai)|1=Yashima Mausoleum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} – Kitanagai-cho, Nara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja|崇道神社 (御所市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Gose)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Gose City
=== Hyōgo Prefecture === * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja|崇導神社 (揖保郡太子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Taishi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Taishi, Ibo County; Main deity: Prince Toneri * {{ill|Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja|崇道天皇神社 (たつの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō Jinja (Tatsuno)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tatsuno City * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja|ja|総道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Shisō City; Main deity: {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiaki|en|Ashikaga Yoshiaki|WD=Q434415|lt=Ashikaga Yoshiaki|1=Ashikaga Yoshiaki}} * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrines]][[ja:崇道天皇神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:崇道天皇神社]]
{{One source|date=January 2024}}
[[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|280px|thumb|Sudo Tenno Shrine, Nishikidera-cho, Nara City]] '''Sudo Tenno Shrine''' (崇道天皇神社, ''Sudō Tennō Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} primarily dedicated to {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (Emperor Sudo), found primarily in Nara and across Western Japan{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}. Alternative names include '''Sudō Tennō-sha''' (崇道天皇社), '''Sudō Jinja''' (崇道神社), and phonetic variations such as '''Sōdō Jinja''' (宗道神社) or '''Sōdo Jinja''' (惣戸神社){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=89}}.
== History == This shrine venerates {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, who died under suspicion of involvement in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|1=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}}. He passed away during exile in {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}, and later was believed to cause calamities. Thus, the shrine has a strong association with the {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=Goryō faith|1=Goryō}}. Prince Sawara was reburied at {{ill|Yashima Tomb|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Tomb|1=Yashima Mausoleum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} in Yamato Province, where a shrine named {{ill|Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|ja|八島陵#崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō-sha (Yashima Tomb)|1=Yashima Mausoleum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875285}} existed until 1886. Nearby smaller shrines are believed to have branched from it{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. The Sudō Tennō-sha in Nishikidera-cho, Nara City, is thought to be established on the former site of the mikoshi hall dedicated to Emperor Sudo during the Nanto Goryō-e (南都御霊会) rituals{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=126}}<ref>璉珹寺紀</ref><ref>{{ill|Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōmoku Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96711987}}</ref>.
In regional areas, particularly in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}}, the Sudō Jinja or Sōdō shrines may originate from granaries built in 805 (Enryaku 24) by imperial order for the appeasement of Emperor Sudo’s spirit. These granaries later became shrines{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki">Nihon Kōki (日本後紀), Enryaku 24</ref><ref name="ruiju">Ruiju Sandai Kyaku (類従三代格)</ref>. These granaries stored 40 bundles of rice annually from each county to appease his spirit{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=120}}<ref name="koki"/><ref name="ruiju"/>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=125}}<ref>Ōkagami</ref><ref>Daianji Sudō Tennō Miin Yashima Ryosho Kibun</ref>.
Some shrines named Sudō Jinja do not enshrine Prince Sawara. For instance, according to the Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho (興福寺官務牒疏, 1441), the Sudō Tennō Jinja in Haji, Sōraku County, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, venerates {{ill|Prince Toneri|en|Prince Toneri|WD=Q1362200|lt=Prince Toneri|1=Prince Toneri}}<ref>Kōfukuji Kanmu Chōsho</ref>{{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}. This may be due to confusion stemming from Prince Toneri’s posthumous title, '''Sudō Jinkei Kōtei''' (崇道尽敬皇帝){{sfn|小さき社の列島史|p=127}}.
== Notable Sudō Tennō Shrines Nationwide == === Ibaraki Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|ja|宗道神社 (下妻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Shimotsuma)|qq=}} – Shimotsuma City; Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Okayama Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja|宗道神社 (津山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Jinja (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Tsuyama City<ref>{{Cite web|title=ありし日の鍛冶職人の心意気を伝える宗道神社|url=https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=100|accessdate=2019-03-08}}</ref> * {{ill|Kamo Jinja|ja|加茂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Jinja|1=Kamo Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398885}} – Tsuyama City (formerly Sugi Daimyōjin-sha, renamed due to nearby tomb) * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja|崇道神社 (井原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Ibara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Ibara City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja|崇道神社 (小田郡矢掛町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Yakage)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Yakage, Oda County
=== Hiroshima Prefecture === * {{ill|Sōdo Jinja|ja|惣戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdo Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja|崇堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Fukuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Oka Shimamori Kagekatsu * {{ill|Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja|惣堂明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Myōjin-sha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiaki|en|Ashikaga Yoshiaki|WD=Q434415|lt=Ashikaga Yoshiaki|1=Ashikaga Yoshiaki}} * {{ill|Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja|上惣堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Sōdō Jinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Fukuyama City; Main deity: Ashikaga Yoshiaki * {{ill|Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja|崇道神社 (神石郡神石高原町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Jinja (Jinseki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jinseki-Kōgen, Jinseki County
